answer:
The video has a resolution of 352 × 288 pixels per frame, with 3 color channels (RGB) and 30 frames per second.  
Each pixel requires 3 bytes (24 bits), so the data rate is 352 × 288 × 24 × 30 bits per second.  
Calculating: 352 × 288 = 101,376; × 24 = 2,433,024; × 30 = 72,990,720 bits/s, which is 73.0 Mbits/s (3 sig. fig.), closest to 73.1.

\boxed{I}
